school: Father of Prophet Muhammad. He married Amina bint Wahb of the Banu Zuhra. He died in Medina during or returning from a trading journey to Syria, either shortly before or just after Muhammad's birth, making the Prophet an orphan from the start. The Quran references this: 'Did He not find you an orphan and shelter you?' (93:6).

books.title: Quran — Sura al-Duha (93:6)
books.note: God's reminder to Muhammad of his orphanhood

school:
Muhammad ibn Abdullah ibn Abd al-Muttalib (570–632 CE) is the final Prophet and Messenger of God in Islam, sent as a mercy to all the worlds (Quran 21:107). Born in Mecca in the Year of the Elephant, orphaned by age six, he was raised by his grandfather Abd al-Muttalib and then his uncle Abu Talib.

Known from youth as al-Amin (the Trustworthy) and al-Sadiq (the Truthful), he worked as a merchant before receiving the first revelation from God through the Angel Jibril in the Cave of Hira at age 40. The first word revealed was ‘Iqra’ (Read) — the opening of Surah al-Alaq.

For thirteen years he preached in Mecca, enduring persecution, boycott, and the loss of his beloved wife Khadijah and uncle Abu Talib in the Year of Sorrow. The Hijra (migration) to Madinah in 622 CE marks the start of the Islamic calendar.

In Madinah he established the first Islamic state, authored the Constitution of Madinah — one of history’s earliest written constitutions — and led the Muslim community through the battles of Badr, Uhud, and the Trench. The Treaty of Hudaybiyyah and the peaceful conquest of Mecca in 630 CE demonstrated his statesmanship.

His Farewell Sermon at Arafat in 632 CE, delivered to over 100,000 people, proclaimed the equality of all humans, the sanctity of life and property, and the completion of God’s message. He passed away in Madinah shortly after, in the arms of his wife Aisha, and is buried in the Prophet’s Mosque.

He left behind the Quran — God’s final revelation — and the Sunnah, his lived example preserved in the hadith literature. Every Muslim scholarly tradition traces its authority back to him. He is the most consequential figure in the history of Islamic civilisation.

books.title: Sahih al-Bukhari
books.note: Most authenticated hadith collection — read online at Sunnah.com

books.title: Sahih Muslim
books.note: Second most authenticated hadith collection — read online at Sunnah.com

parents: 'Abdullah ibn 'Abd al-Muttalib / Amina bint Wahb b 'Abd Munaf bin Zuhrah
children: al-Qasim bin Muhammad , Zaynab bint Muhammad , Ruqayyah bint Muhammad , Umm Kulthum bint Muhammad , Fatima bint Muhammad , 'Abdullah bin Muhammad , Ibrahim bin Muhammad
spouses: Khadijah , Sawda bint Zam'a , 'Aisha bint Abi Bakr , Hafsa bint Umar , Zaynab bint Khazeemah , Umm Salamah , Zaynab bint Jahsh , Juwayriya bint al-Harith , Umm Habibah bint Abi Sufyan , Safiyya bint Huyayy , Maymuna bint al-Harith , Maria al-Qibtiyya
quotes: The best of you are those who are best to their families.
God does not look at your forms and possessions, but He looks at your hearts and your deeds.
The strong person is not the one who can wrestle someone else down. The strong person is the one who can control himself when he is angry.
None of you truly believes until he wishes for his brother what he wishes for himself.
Make things easy, do not make them difficult. Give glad tidings, do not drive people away.
The ink of the scholar is holier than the blood of the martyr.
Whoever is not merciful to others will not be treated mercifully.
A white person has no superiority over a black person, nor a black person over a white — except by piety and good action.
Leave that which makes you doubt for that which does not make you doubt.
quranDetail: Mentioned by name: Ali Imran 3:144, Al-Ahzab 33:40, Muhammad 47:2, Al-Fath 48:29. Called Ahmad: As-Saff 61:6. Key verses about him: Al-Anbiya 21:107 (mercy to the worlds), Al-Qalam 68:4 (exalted character), Al-Ahzab 33:21 (beautiful example), Al-Fath 48:29 (those with him), At-Tawbah 9:128 (concerned for believers), Al-Duha 93:1-11 (God’s comfort), Al-Inshirah 94:1-8 (expansion of the heart), Al-Kawthar 108:1-3 (abundance), An-Najm 53:1-18 (the Night Journey). Entire surahs addressed to him include Al-Muzzammil (73), Al-Muddaththir (74), and Surah Muhammad (47).

school: Al-Qarawiyyin — Founded 859 CE in Fez; fasted throughout 2-year construction; produced Ibn Rushd, Ibn Khaldun, Maimonides

Abd Allah ibn Abd al-Muttalib was the father of Prophet Muhammad and the son of Abd al-Muttalib ibn Hashim, chief of the Quraysh. He died before the Prophet's birth, leaving his son an orphan. His marriage to Aminah bint Wahb produced the last and greatest Prophet of Islam.
